comment_text.sn12.64:2.1: Consciousness is organic, established or planted like a seed, for which see also SN 22.54, AN 3.76], [AN 3.77.
comment_text.sn12.64:2.3: As an individual matures, they develop the capacity to make responsible moral decisions.
comment_text.sn12.64:2.4: Here choices in this life directly beget rebirth, which is normally stated as “consciousness”.
comment_text.sn12.64:8.8: If a beam of light does not land anywhere, it cannot perform its key function, namely to illuminate. Likewise, if consciousness is not established in craving, it cannot perform its key function of perpetuating the cycle of rebirth.

root_text.sn12.64:1.2: “Cattārome, bhikkhave, āhārā bhūtānaṁ vā sattānaṁ ṭhitiyā sambhavesīnaṁ vā anuggahāya.
root_text.sn12.64:1.4: Kabaḷīkāro āhāro oḷāriko vā sukhumo vā, phasso dutiyo, manosañcetanā tatiyā, viññāṇaṁ catutthaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:1.5: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro āhārā bhūtānaṁ vā sattānaṁ ṭhitiyā sambhavesīnaṁ vā anuggahāya.
root_text.sn12.64:2.1: Kabaḷīkāre ce, bhikkhave, āhāre atthi rāgo atthi nandī atthi taṇhā, patiṭṭhitaṁ tattha viññāṇaṁ virūḷhaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:2.2: Yattha patiṭṭhitaṁ viññāṇaṁ virūḷhaṁ, atthi tattha nāmarūpassa avakkanti.
root_text.sn12.64:2.3: Yattha atthi nāmarūpassa avakkanti, atthi tattha saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi.
root_text.sn12.64:2.4: Yattha atthi saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi, atthi tattha āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti.
root_text.sn12.64:2.5: Yattha atthi āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti, atthi tattha āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:2.6: Yattha atthi āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ, sasokaṁ taṁ, bhikkhave, sadaraṁ saupāyāsanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn12.64:3.3: viññāṇe ce, bhikkhave, āhāre atthi rāgo atthi nandī atthi taṇhā, patiṭṭhitaṁ tattha viññāṇaṁ virūḷhaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:3.4: Yattha patiṭṭhitaṁ viññāṇaṁ virūḷhaṁ, atthi tattha nāmarūpassa avakkanti.
root_text.sn12.64:3.5: Yattha atthi nāmarūpassa avakkanti, atthi tattha saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi.
root_text.sn12.64:3.6: Yattha atthi saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi, atthi tattha āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti.
root_text.sn12.64:3.7: Yattha atthi āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti, atthi tattha āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:3.8: Yattha atthi āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ, sasokaṁ taṁ, bhikkhave, sadaraṁ saupāyāsanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn12.64:4.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, rajako vā cittakārako vā sati rajanāya vā lākhāya vā haliddiyā vā nīliyā vā mañjiṭṭhāya vā suparimaṭṭhe vā phalake bhittiyā vā dussapaṭṭe vā itthirūpaṁ vā purisarūpaṁ vā abhinimmineyya sabbaṅgapaccaṅgaṁ;
root_text.sn12.64:4.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, kabaḷīkāre ce āhāre atthi rāgo atthi nandī atthi taṇhā, patiṭṭhitaṁ tattha viññāṇaṁ virūḷhaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:4.3: Yattha patiṭṭhitaṁ viññāṇaṁ virūḷhaṁ, atthi tattha nāmarūpassa avakkanti.
root_text.sn12.64:4.4: Yattha atthi nāmarūpassa avakkanti, atthi tattha saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi.
root_text.sn12.64:4.5: Yattha atthi saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi, atthi tattha āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti.
root_text.sn12.64:4.6: Yattha atthi āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti, atthi tattha āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:4.7: Yattha atthi āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ, sasokaṁ taṁ, bhikkhave, sadaraṁ saupāyāsanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn12.64:5.3: viññāṇe ce, bhikkhave, āhāre atthi rāgo atthi nandī atthi taṇhā, patiṭṭhitaṁ tattha viññāṇaṁ virūḷhaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:5.4: Yattha patiṭṭhitaṁ viññāṇaṁ virūḷhaṁ, atthi tattha nāmarūpassa avakkanti.
root_text.sn12.64:5.5: Yattha atthi nāmarūpassa avakkanti, atthi tattha saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi.
root_text.sn12.64:5.6: Yattha atthi saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi, atthi tattha āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti.
root_text.sn12.64:5.7: Yattha atthi āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti, atthi tattha āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:5.8: Yattha atthi āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ, sasokaṁ taṁ, bhikkhave, sadaraṁ saupāyāsanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn12.64:6.1: Kabaḷīkāre ce, bhikkhave, āhāre natthi rāgo natthi nandī natthi taṇhā, appatiṭṭhitaṁ tattha viññāṇaṁ avirūḷhaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:6.2: Yattha appatiṭṭhitaṁ viññāṇaṁ avirūḷhaṁ, natthi tattha nāmarūpassa avakkanti.
root_text.sn12.64:6.3: Yattha natthi nāmarūpassa avakkanti, natthi tattha saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi.
root_text.sn12.64:6.4: Yattha natthi saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi, natthi tattha āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti.
root_text.sn12.64:6.5: Yattha natthi āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti, natthi tattha āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:6.6: Yattha natthi āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ, asokaṁ taṁ, bhikkhave, adaraṁ anupāyāsanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn12.64:7.3: viññāṇe ce, bhikkhave, āhāre natthi rāgo natthi nandī natthi taṇhā, appatiṭṭhitaṁ tattha viññāṇaṁ avirūḷhaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:7.4: Yattha appatiṭṭhitaṁ viññāṇaṁ avirūḷhaṁ, natthi tattha nāmarūpassa avakkanti.
root_text.sn12.64:7.5: Yattha natthi nāmarūpassa avakkanti, natthi tattha saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi.
root_text.sn12.64:7.6: Yattha natthi saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi, natthi tattha āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti.
root_text.sn12.64:7.7: Yattha natthi āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti, natthi tattha āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:7.8: Yattha natthi āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ, asokaṁ taṁ, bhikkhave, adaraṁ anupāyāsanti vadāmi.
root_text.sn12.64:8.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, kūṭāgāraṁ vā kūṭāgārasālaṁ vā uttarāya vā dakkhiṇāya vā pācīnāya vā vātapānā sūriye uggacchante vātapānena rasmi pavisitvā kvāssa patiṭṭhitā”ti?
root_text.sn12.64:8.3: “Pacchimā ce, bhikkhave, bhitti nāssa kvāssa patiṭṭhitā”ti?
root_text.sn12.64:8.9: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, kabaḷīkāre ce āhāre natthi rāgo natthi nandī natthi taṇhā …pe….
root_text.sn12.64:9.3: viññāṇe ce, bhikkhave, āhāre natthi rāgo natthi nandī natthi taṇhā, appatiṭṭhitaṁ tattha viññāṇaṁ avirūḷhaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:9.4: Yattha appatiṭṭhitaṁ viññāṇaṁ avirūḷhaṁ, natthi tattha nāmarūpassa avakkanti.
root_text.sn12.64:9.5: Yattha natthi nāmarūpassa avakkanti, natthi tattha saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi.
root_text.sn12.64:9.6: Yattha natthi saṅkhārānaṁ vuddhi, natthi tattha āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti.
root_text.sn12.64:9.7: Yattha natthi āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti, natthi tattha āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn12.64:9.8: Yattha natthi āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇaṁ asokaṁ taṁ, bhikkhave, adaraṁ anupāyāsanti vadāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn12.64:1.2: “Mendicants, these four fuels maintain sentient beings that have been born and help those about to be born.
translation_text.sn12.64:1.4: Edible food, whether solid or subtle; contact is the second, mental intention the third, and consciousness the fourth.
translation_text.sn12.64:1.5: These are the four fuels that maintain sentient beings that have been born and help those about to be born.
translation_text.sn12.64:2.1: If there is desire, relishing, and craving for edible food, consciousness becomes established there and grows.
translation_text.sn12.64:2.2: Where consciousness is established and grows, name and form are conceived.
translation_text.sn12.64:2.3: Where name and form are conceived, there is the growth of choices.
translation_text.sn12.64:2.4: Where choices grow, there is regeneration into a new state of existence in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:2.5: Where there is regeneration into a new state of existence in the future, there is rebirth, old age, and death in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:2.6: Where there is rebirth, old age, and death in the future, I say this is full of sorrow, anguish, and distress.
translation_text.sn12.64:3.1: If there is desire, relishing, and craving for contact as fuel …
translation_text.sn12.64:3.2: If there is desire, relishing, and craving for mental intention as fuel …
translation_text.sn12.64:3.3: If there is desire, relishing, and craving for consciousness as fuel, consciousness becomes established there and grows.
translation_text.sn12.64:3.4: Where consciousness is established and grows, name and form are conceived.
translation_text.sn12.64:3.5: Where name and form are conceived, there is the growth of choices.
translation_text.sn12.64:3.6: Where choices grow, there is regeneration into a new state of existence in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:3.7: Where there is regeneration into a new state of existence in the future, there is rebirth, old age, and death in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:3.8: Where there is rebirth, old age, and death in the future, I say this is full of sorrow, anguish, and distress.
translation_text.sn12.64:4.1: Suppose an artist or painter had some dye, red lac, turmeric, indigo, or rose madder. And on a polished plank or a wall or a canvas they’d create the form of a woman or a man, whole in its major and minor limbs.
translation_text.sn12.64:4.2: In the same way, if there is desire, relishing, and craving for edible food, consciousness becomes established there and grows.
translation_text.sn12.64:4.3: Where consciousness is established and grows, name and form are conceived.
translation_text.sn12.64:4.4: Where name and form are conceived, there is the growth of choices.
translation_text.sn12.64:4.5: Where choices grow, there is regeneration into a new state of existence in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:4.6: Where there is regeneration into a new state of existence in the future, there is rebirth, old age, and death in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:4.7: Where there is rebirth, old age, and death in the future, I say this is full of sorrow, anguish, and distress.
translation_text.sn12.64:5.1: If there is desire, relishing, and craving for contact as fuel …
translation_text.sn12.64:5.2: If there is desire, relishing, and craving for mental intention as fuel …
translation_text.sn12.64:5.3: If there is desire, relishing, and craving for consciousness as fuel, consciousness becomes established there and grows.
translation_text.sn12.64:5.4: Where consciousness is established and grows, name and form are conceived.
translation_text.sn12.64:5.5: Where name and form are conceived, there is the growth of choices.
translation_text.sn12.64:5.6: Where choices grow, there is regeneration into a new state of existence in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:5.7: Where there is regeneration into a new state of existence in the future, there is rebirth, old age, and death in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:5.8: Where there is rebirth, old age, and death in the future, I say this is full of sorrow, anguish, and distress.
translation_text.sn12.64:6.1: If there is no desire, relishing, and craving for edible food, consciousness does not become established there and doesn’t grow.
translation_text.sn12.64:6.2: Where consciousness is not established and doesn’t grow, name and form are not conceived.
translation_text.sn12.64:6.3: Where name and form are not conceived, there is no growth of choices.
translation_text.sn12.64:6.4: Where choices don’t grow, there is no regeneration into a new state of existence in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:6.5: Where there is no regeneration into a new state of existence in the future, there is no rebirth, old age, and death in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:6.6: Where there is no rebirth, old age, and death in the future, I say there’s no sorrow, anguish, and distress.
translation_text.sn12.64:7.1: If there is no desire, relishing, and craving for contact as fuel …
translation_text.sn12.64:7.2: If there is no desire, relishing, and craving for mental intention as fuel …
translation_text.sn12.64:7.3: If there is no desire, relishing, and craving for consciousness as fuel, consciousness doesn’t become established there and doesn’t grow.
translation_text.sn12.64:7.4: Where consciousness is not established and doesn’t grow, name and form are not conceived.
translation_text.sn12.64:7.5: Where name and form are not conceived, there is no growth of choices.
translation_text.sn12.64:7.6: Where choices don’t grow, there is no regeneration into a new state of existence in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:7.7: Where there is no regeneration into a new state of existence in the future, there is no rebirth, old age, and death in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:7.8: Where there is no rebirth, old age, and death in the future, I say there’s no sorrow, anguish, and distress.
translation_text.sn12.64:8.1: Suppose there was a bungalow or a hall with a peaked roof, with windows on the northern, southern, or eastern side. When the sun rises and a ray of light enters through a window, where would it land?”
translation_text.sn12.64:8.3: “If there was no western wall, where would it land?”
translation_text.sn12.64:8.5: “If there was no ground, where would it land?”
translation_text.sn12.64:8.7: “If there was no water, where would it land?”
translation_text.sn12.64:8.9: “In the same way, if there is no desire, relishing, and craving for edible food, consciousness does not become established there and doesn’t grow. …
translation_text.sn12.64:9.1: If there is no desire, relishing, and craving for contact as fuel …
translation_text.sn12.64:9.2: If there is no desire, relishing, and craving for mental intention as fuel …
translation_text.sn12.64:9.3: If there is no desire, relishing, and craving for consciousness as fuel, consciousness doesn’t become established there and doesn’t grow.
translation_text.sn12.64:9.4: Where consciousness is not established and doesn’t grow, name and form are not conceived.
translation_text.sn12.64:9.5: Where name and form are not conceived, there is no growth of choices.
translation_text.sn12.64:9.6: Where choices don’t grow, there is no regeneration into a new state of existence in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:9.7: Where there is no regeneration into a new state of existence in the future, there is no rebirth, old age, and death in the future.
translation_text.sn12.64:9.8: Where there is no rebirth, old age, and death in the future, I say there’s no sorrow, anguish, and distress.”

variant_text.sn12.64:8.1: kvāssa patiṭṭhitā → kvassa patiṭṭhitā (pts1ed, pts2ed); kattha patiṭṭhitā (mr)

reference_text.sn12.64:1.1: bj14.158, csp1ed12.325, csp2ed12.325, ms12S2_456, msdiv64, pts-vp-pli2.101
